summary: Implemented two previously placeholder functions: `lbAudioAx_80024DC4` in the audio library code and `mnName_8023749C` in name-entry menu code. The audio match depended on recognizing `lbl_80433710` as an extended pool object with an embedded `HSD_ObjAllocData` plus sound-channel arrays. The menu function became a typed, language-specific name-table lookup; the PR body describes terminator appending, but the diff evidence shows lookup-and-NULL-on-terminator behavior. Automated feedback confirmed `lbAudioAx_80024DC4` as a new 100% match, while the same bot report listed `mnName_8023749C` as 97.84%, conflicting with the PR body's 100% fuzzy-match claim.

key_files.reason: Implemented `lbAudioAx_80024DC4` and updated allocator API calls to pass `&lbl_80433710.alloc`.
Replaced `static HSD_ObjAllocData lbl_80433710` with `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc`, exposing embedded allocator and arrays at offsets 0x2C and 0x70.
Implemented `mnName_8023749C`, added `lb/lblanguage.h`, extern name tables, and scoped `#pragma opt_common_subs off`.
Changed `mnName_8023749C` prototype from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to `char* mnName_8023749C(int slot)`.

what_changed: `lbAudioAx_80024DC4(int arg0)` now scans 16 entries in `lbl_80433710.x2C`; if `arg0` is already present it refreshes `lbl_80433710.x70[i]` to `0xA` and returns.
If the audio ID is not already present, `lbAudioAx_80024DC4` scans for sentinel value `0x83D60U`, writes `arg0` into `x2C[i]`, sets `x70[i]` to `0xA`, and returns.
`lbl_80433710` was retyped as `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc` with `HSD_ObjAllocData alloc` at 0x00, `s32 x2C[17]` at 0x2C, and `s32 x70[17]` at 0x70, total noted size 0xB4.
`HSD_ObjFree`, `HSD_ObjAlloc`, and `HSD_ObjAllocInit` call sites were adjusted from `&lbl_80433710` to `&lbl_80433710.alloc` after the BSS symbol became a wrapper struct.

`mnName_8023749C(int slot)` now chooses `mnNameNew_803EE724` for saved US language and `mnNameNew_803EE720` otherwise, walks until the requested slot or `mnName_StringTerminator`, and returns NULL if the selected entry begins with the terminator.
`mnName_8023749C` was wrapped in `#pragma push`, `#pragma opt_common_subs off`, and `#pragma pop` for local codegen control.
`mnname.h` now records the concrete return and argument types for `mnName_8023749C`.
smart_moves: Recognized that a BSS symbol used by HSD allocation APIs was not only an `HSD_ObjAllocData`, but a larger subsystem state object whose first member is the allocator.
Kept allocator compatibility by passing `&lbl_80433710.alloc` to HSD functions instead of forcing the whole wrapper struct into allocator APIs.
Modeled the discovered arrays as 17 entries even though the active loops use a 0x10 bound, preserving offsets and total BSS layout.
Used a narrowly scoped compiler pragma around only `mnName_8023749C`, avoiding broader optimization side effects.
Matched compare behavior with explicit signedness casts such as `(u32) q[i] == 0x83D60U`, `(s8) mnName_StringTerminator`, and `(u8) slot`.
Replaced an unknown prototype with `char* mnName_8023749C(int slot)`, improving type information for future callers.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume a static symbol passed to `HSD_ObjAlloc` is exactly an `HSD_ObjAllocData`; it may be the first field of a larger module-owned state struct.
When expanding a global into a wrapper struct, update all library call sites to pass the embedded member address, not the wrapper address.
Array extents in static structs should be chosen from offsets and BSS layout, not only from loop trip counts.
Small codegen details like duplicate locals pointing at the same array, empty loop bodies, unsigned sentinel constants, and signed char casts can be required for matching.
Use `#pragma opt_common_subs off` as a surgical tactic for functions whose match is disrupted by common-subexpression optimization.
Treat PR-body semantic summaries cautiously when they conflict with the diff; here the body says name terminator append, while the implemented function returns a name-table pointer or NULL.
naming_conventions: Address-style names were retained for still-unnamed functions and data: `lbAudioAx_80024DC4`, `mnName_8023749C`, `lbl_80433710`, `mnNameNew_803EE720`, and `mnNameNew_803EE724`.
The new struct name `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc` follows subsystem-plus-role naming.
Unknown struct fields were named by offset, `x2C` and `x70`, while the known embedded allocator field was named `alloc`.
Subsystem prefixes remain consistent: `lbAudioAx_` for audio-library functions, `mnName_` for name menu functions, and `lbLang_` for language selection.
Header declarations should be upgraded from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` once return and parameter types are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used two local pointers, `p` and `q`, both initialized to `st->x2C`, matching the two-loop audio implementation shape.
Used separate 0x10-bound loops for existing-sound refresh and free-slot claim instead of combining the scans.
Compared free slots with `(u32) q[i] == 0x83D60U` to preserve unsigned constant/codegen behavior.
Used explicit `(s8)` casts for terminator and string first-byte checks and `(u8) slot` in the name-table loop condition.
Preserved ABI/layout by changing HSD allocator calls to use the embedded `alloc` field.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 58.73%, +140 bytes overall.
Bot report listed `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` / `lbAudioAx_80024DC4` as a new match, +140 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
Bot report listed `.bss` in `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` improving from 0.24% to 44.44%.
Bot report listed `main/melee/mn/mnname` / `mnName_8023749C` as +172 bytes, 0.00% to 97.84%, which conflicts with the PR body's `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions` statement.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `mnName_8023749C` to see whether the bot-reported 97.84% mismatch was resolved.
Search later changes to `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc`, `lbl_80433710.x2C`, and `lbl_80433710.x70` for field renames or confirmed semantics.
Look for other BSS symbols where an `HSD_ObjAllocData` is the first member of a larger static allocator/state struct.
Search for `0x83D60U` and audio-pool sentinel usage to name the free-slot marker and related fields.
Search for `#pragma opt_common_subs off` in menu/name code for reusable matching patterns.
